data.title: SlowMist Warning: Hackers use fake video calls to carry out social engineering attacks, users need to be vigilant
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News SlowMist Security Team issued a security alert, warning that social engineering attacks with malware have surged again recently. Hackers trick victims into downloading malicious scripts through fake video call links to steal sensitive information.
In the latest case, the attacker first hacked into the Telegram account of the victim's friend, and then used the account to initiate a fake video call to reduce the target's vigilance. The SlowMist team reminds users that if they are attacked, they should take the following measures immediately:
1: Protect assets: If the wallet private key/mnemonic phrase has been stored on the computer, the funds should be transferred as soon as possible.
2: Change password & 2FA: Update Telegram, X, email, exchange and other accounts immediately, and check for abnormal login records.
3: Run full disk antivirus: Use well-known antivirus software such as AVG, Bitdefender, Kaspersky, etc. to scan the system.
4: Reset the system (if necessary): Back up important files, reinstall the system and ensure that the virus has been thoroughly checked before recovery.
Users should be vigilant and avoid clicking on unknown video call links to ensure the safety of their assets.

data.title: The whale that once made a profit of $9.28 million once again opened a 50x ETH long order on Hyperliquid
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, the whale that previously made a profit of $9.28 million in Hyperliquid's 50x leveraged trading has once again increased its bet to open a 50x ETH long order. 
 2 minutes ago, the whale recharged 3.485 million USDC as margin
Current holdings: 17,130 ETH (about 31.2 million US dollars)
Average opening price: 1,863.62 US dollars
Liquidation price: 1,677.1 US dollars
Current floating profit: 80,000 US dollars

data.title: Security Alert: Surge In Malware Attacks Through Fake Video Calls
data.description:
According to Odaily, the SlowMist security team has issued a warning about a recent surge in social engineering attacks involving malware. Hackers are using fake video call links to trick victims into downloading malicious scripts, which are then used to steal sensitive information. In recent incidents, attackers first compromised the Telegram accounts of victims' friends and then used these accounts to initiate fake video calls, reducing the target's suspicion.

The SlowMist team advises users to take immediate action if they encounter such attacks. Recommended steps include:

1. Protecting Assets: If a wallet's private key or mnemonic phrase was stored on a computer, funds should be transferred promptly.

2. Changing Passwords and Enabling 2FA: Users should immediately update passwords for accounts such as Telegram, X, email, and exchanges, and check for any unusual login activity.

3. Running a Full Antivirus Scan: Utilize reputable antivirus software like AVG, Bitdefender, or Kaspersky to scan the system.

4. System Reset (if necessary): Backup important files, reinstall the operating system, and ensure the system is virus-free before restoring data.

Users are urged to remain vigilant, avoid clicking on unknown video call links, and ensure the security of their assets.

data.title: Cobo CEO Reports Significant Asset Growth Since '312' Event
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on March 12, Cobo Co-Founder and CEO Shenyu initiated a five-year asset scale survey marking the anniversary of the '312' event. The data reveals that assets have increased significantly, ranging from 15 to 100 times since the event, with the primary source of wealth being Bitcoin accumulation.

On March 12, 2020, Bitcoin's price plummeted to $3,800. Five years later, it reached a peak of $110,000 and is currently priced at $81,700, marking a more than 20-fold increase over the five-year period.

data.title: Trump's Tariff War Could Force a Fed Rate Cut—Will Crypto Markets Rebound?
data.description:
Trump's escalating tariff war might potentially pressure the Federal Reserve into implementing an interest rate cut—could such monetary policy changes trigger a rebound in cryptocurrency markets?
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/latest-news/trump-traffic-war-crypto-rebound

data.title: The suspected Longling Capital address added another 10,003 ETH one hour ago, equivalent to about 18.65 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of Ai Yi, an on-chain analyst, "the giant whale/institution that bought ETH low and sold it high and made a profit of 33.67 million US dollars" added another 10,003 ETH an hour ago, worth 18.65 million US dollars. So far, the number of ETH it has built since December 2024 has increased to 54,005 (with a total value of 131 million US dollars). The batch-by-batch increase has reduced the cost to 2,433 US dollars. The current floating loss is 30.48 million US dollars. The address may belong to Longling Capital.

data.title: LBank launches $100 million risk protection fund, with 120% compensation for contract plugging and additional airdrop of 10,000 USDT
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, in order to improve the fairness of transactions, LBank has officially established a $100 million contract risk protection fund and launched a double compensation mechanism for plug-in incidents. If a user suffers forced liquidation or stop loss due to plug-in in a contract transaction, LBank will pay 120% of the loss amount to minimize the user's loss. The compensation amount will be paid to the user's spot account in the form of USDT within 48 hours. At the same time, after each plug-in incident, LBank will also airdrop an additional 10,000 USDT to all position holders according to the position ratio to ensure that users who have not lost money can also share the benefits.
It is reported that this event applies to the top 100 contract trading pairs ranked by CoinMarketCap market value. This initiative aims to optimize the user's trading experience, reduce the risks brought by abnormal market fluctuations, and allow every user to participate in contract trading in a safer and more reliable environment.

data.title: Trump officially slaps 25% tariffs on all aluminum and steel imports, EU responds with counter tariffs
data.description:
Trump has officially imposed 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports, ending the 2018 exemptions. The move underlines his February plan to reduce foreign imports into the country to promote US factory jobs. AP News reported that these tariffs will go into effect for Canada, Mexico, China, the EU, Brazil, and South Korea. Earlier, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-tariffs-aluminum-and-steel-imports/

data.title: Jsquare announces the official launch of the first phase of the $50 million LP fund Pioneer Fund
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News During ETHDenver in the United States, Jsquare announced the official launch of the first phase of its first LP fund, Pioneer Fund, with a fund size of US$50 million, focusing on investing in Web3 tracks such as AI, consumer applications, RWA, DeFi, and PayFi.
Founding Partner Joanna said that the fund will focus on open and compliant operations, seize market expansion trends, and support the development of projects in high-potential fields.

data.title: PIMCO: Tariffs raise U.S. recession chances to 35%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Alec Kersman, managing director and head of Asia Pacific at Pacific Investment Management Company (PIMCO), said that due to the US tariff measures, the possibility of the United States falling into a recession this year has increased, and he estimated the probability to be 35%. This probability is higher than the 15% assessed by Pimco in December last year. Despite this, Pimco's basic forecast for the US economy is to grow by 1% to 1.5%, although it has "fallen sharply" from previous expectations. Kamal Bhatia, president and CEO of Principal Asset Management, said that such trade policies will promote domestic consumption and help the US economy grow higher than expected. Consumer spending on goods and services accounts for about two-thirds of the US GDP. Bhatia said that the increase in domestic spending caused by tariffs is "highly likely" to drive the US GDP to "perform better than expected." (Jinshi)
